Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Anh Nguyen's resignation email using search_emails. Discovers email from anh.nguyen@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com (CC elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com) with subject "Resignation Notice - Last Day May 16" detailing Anh's active work status: ENG-218 (mostly complete, needs QA + Lena review), ENG-213 (bulk export CSV functional, policy version history UI is a stub scaffolded in /src/dashboard/PolicyHistory.tsx but data fetching and diff visualization not built), ENG-203 backlog (notification preferences, batch operations, mobile-responsive layout none started), and uncommitted local branches (dark mode toggle, accessibility audit).
OE 2: List all Linear issues on the expense tool project using linear_list_projects then linear_list_issues with project "proj_expense_autocategorizer". Discovers the full set of tickets under proj_expense_autocategorizer: ENG-201 (Core Engine epic, state_backlog), ENG-202 (Policy Config epic, state_backlog), ENG-203 (Admin Dashboard epic, state_backlog, unassigned), ENG-204 (Integration Testing epic, state_backlog), ENG-205 (CategoryMatcher, state_todo, Lena), ENG-206 (PolicyValidator, state_todo, Lena), ENG-207 (DecisionEngine, state_todo, Lena), ENG-208 (Rule config UI, state_todo, Anh), ENG-209 (Policy ingestion, state_todo, Samira), ENG-210 (ML v2 backlog, state_backlog, Dmitri), ENG-211 (Pilot monitoring, state_in_progress, Samira), ENG-212 (Edge cases, state_in_progress, Dmitri), ENG-213 (Bulk export, state_backlog, Anh), ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, state_in_progress, Lena), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, state_in_progress, Lena), ENG-216 (Full audit of pilot expenses, state_in_progress, Dmitri), ENG-217 (Pipeline hardening, state_in_progress, Lena), ENG-218 (Dashboard type validation, state_in_progress, Anh), ENG-219 (Launch readiness checklist, state_todo, Samira), ENG-220 (Duplicate detection, state_todo, Dmitri), ENG-221 (Staging cost guardrails, state_todo, unassigned).
OE 3: Read comments on ENG-203 (issue_admin_dashboard) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_admin_dashboard". Finds Anh's comment (comment_anh_dashboard_done, 2026-02-27) confirming the dashboard was delivered to staging with core features complete: expense review queue, manual override, audit trail, and rule configuration UI. Bulk export and policy version history still outstanding. This confirms ENG-208 (rule config UI) was completed as part of the dashboard.
OE 4: Read comments on ENG-202 (issue_policy_config) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_policy_config". Finds Lena's comment (comment_lena_engine_staging, 2026-02-27) confirming the Policy Config epic is complete with all three sub-components developed and operational end-to-end in staging: ENG-205 CategoryMatcher (keyword scoring with multi-word phrase prioritization, 7 standard categories plus other fallback), ENG-206 PolicyValidator (per-client JSON config loading with category and limit enforcement), ENG-207 DecisionEngine (approval/denial/escalation routing). All three tickets remain in state_todo despite being completed, making them stale.
OE 5: Read comments on ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed". Finds Lena's deployment verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified) confirming config corrections deployed and verified in production with specific test cases: Danielle Osei $475 chair approved correctly, Kevin Tran $185 internet approved correctly, Andre Washington $1,200 desk correctly denied, Andre Washington $350 coworking correctly denied. Also confirms auto-decisions are disabled so all cases route to review queue. Lena notes she is closing ENG-214 and starting on pipeline hardening (ENG-217).
OE 6: Read comments on ENG-211 (issue_pilot_monitoring) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_pilot_monitoring". Finds Samira's pause confirmation comment (comment_samira_pause_confirmed, 2026-03-18) confirming auto-decisions disabled for both Vectral and Mosaic, with remediation plan listing ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-212, ENG-219 and earliest re-enablement week of 3/30.
OE 7: Read comments on ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_edge_cases_backlog". Finds Dmitri's validation test comment (comment_dmitri_validation_tests, 2026-03-20) listing six schema validation tests implemented in the ENG-212 branch: test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback. All pass with corrected configs but fail with original configs. Edge case tests for categorization and vendor abbreviations remain.
OE 8: Read comments on ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_audit_expenses". Finds Dmitri's duplicate discovery comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery, 2026-03-15) identifying three flagged stipend records during the post-pause data quality audit: Kevin Tran $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard) and Andre Washington $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard) identical SHA-256 hash (a3f7c8d1e9b2...); Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) different hash but same PDF template origin (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). Total questionable amount: $223 ($89 already paid for Kevin Tran on 2026-03-08, $134 pending).
OE 9: Read comments on ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection". Finds Dmitri's context comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context, 2026-03-15) linking the ticket to the #engineering discussion and Elena's policy directive in #executive. Notes that audit findings are recorded in the ENG-216 comment thread, lists identified Airtable records (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand), QuickBooks holds placed by Marcus on STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097, and AM verification requests sent to Vectral (Mina), Mosaic (Emeka), and BrightLoop (Emeka). Dmitri plans to begin Layer 1 hash dedup implementation.
OE 10: Read comments on ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_pipeline_hardening" to find Dmitri's audit results (comment_dmitri_audit_results). Discovers 47 expenses reviewed: 39 correct (83%), 5 false rejections (all Vectral Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet, Maria Santos $550 desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 WiFi), 3 false approvals (all Mosaic Andre Washington $1,200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking). $885 total exposure.
OE 11: Search Slack channel #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) for Lena's root cause message using conversations_history with channel_id "C009" (or conversations_search_messages). Discovers the March AWS cost spike was caused by the ExpenseBot staging environment running the S3 receipt hash computation job from ENG-220 Layer 1 continuously for 5+ weeks after the pilot pause on March 18. Total excess: ~$12,000 (S3 GETs ~$4,200, DynamoDB writes ~$2,800, data transfer ~$1,600, EC2 compute ~$3,400). Staging now terminated. Deeper issue: no cost alerts or auto-shutdown on staging environments.
OE 12: Search Slack channel #engineering (C003) for Samira's capacity planning message using conversations_history with channel_id "C003" (or conversations_search_messages). Discovers message summarizing three converging issues: (1) AWS spike resolved, need staging cost guardrails added to ENG-219 checklist; (2) Anh leaving May 16 impacts ENG-218, ENG-213, ENG-203; (3) engineering capacity crunch 3-person team handling ExpenseBot relaunch, Terraform onboarding, invoice safety checks, handoff, and staging guardrails. Emergency capacity meeting called for 3 PM Pacific.
OE 13: Search Slack #engineering (C003) and #executive (C004) for Dmitri's stipend duplicate audit finding and Elena's policy directive using conversations_history or conversations_search_messages. Discovers Dmitri's message in C003 reporting three suspicious receipts from TechComfort Supply: Kevin Tran $89 and Andre Washington $89 (identical SHA-256 hash), Jordan Ekwueme $45 (same PDF template). Samira replied confirming hash-based deduplication before CategoryMatcher is the right approach. Dmitri then filed ENG-220. Also discovers Elena's directive in #executive (C004) mandating manual receipt verification for all stipend reimbursement requests over $100 effective immediately, no automatic approvals, AMs must confirm with client HR and record verification in Airtable before finance processes payment.
OE 14: Search emails for Marcus's AWS budget impact email using search_emails. Discovers email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com (CC elena.rostova@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) with subject "AWS Staging Overspend - Budget Impact and Engineering Headcount Timing" flagging three concerns: (1) $12,000 excess = 67% of $18,000 quarterly budget, needs Elena's approval or project allocation by April 28; (2) staging cost controls needed January had a $2,300 spike too; (3) Anh's headcount slot fundable only if req initiated before June 1.
OE 15: Search emails for the expense audit summary and Mosaic false approval alerts using search_emails. Discovers: (a) Samira's audit summary to Elena 47 expenses, 83% accuracy, 5 false rejections (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic), $885 total exposure; (b) Emeka's alert to Samira and Marcus about Mosaic false approvals Andre Washington $1,200 desk ($400 over $800 cap) and $350 coworking (excluded), Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking; (c) Dmitri's email to Marcus about suspicious receipts and Marcus's reply with QuickBooks statuses STIP-2026-0089 ($89, PAID), STIP-2026-0094 ($89, held), STIP-2026-0097 ($45, held); (d) Marcus's verification request emails to Mina (Vectral/Kevin Tran) and Emeka (Mosaic/Andre Washington, BrightLoop/Jordan Ekwueme).
OE 16: Search Airtable for stipend records flagged during the pilot audit using airtable_list_bases, then airtable_list_tables with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", then airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Stipend Transactions", field_name "Account", value "Mosaic Robotics" (and separately "Vectral Systems"), or airtable_list_records to retrieve all records. Finds Mosaic false approvals still showing "Approved": (a) Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk (recStip000000021, "Approved") Mosaic caps at $800, $400 over; (b) Andre Washington $350 coworking (recStip000000022, "Approved") excluded category; (c) Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking (rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork / recStip000000023, "Approved") same exclusion bug. Finds Vectral false rejections still uncorrected: (d) Maria Santos $550 desk (rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk "Pending Approval" / recStip000000018 "Rejected") should be approved, Vectral allows $600; (e) James Okafor $82 keyboard tray (recStip000000019, "Rejected") double bug, pending correction; (f) Lisa Park $130 WiFi (rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi "Pending Approval" / recStip000000020 "Rejected") should be approved, Vectral allows $150. Confirms Airtable stipend data has not been corrected to reflect proper approval decisions despite the audit being finished.
OE 17: Search Airtable for the duplicate-flagged stipend records using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Stipend Transactions". Finds rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard ($89, "Approved", flagged duplicate hash already paid 2026-03-08), rec_stipend_andre_keyboard ($89, "Pending Approval", held by finance STIP-2026-0094), rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand ($45, "Pending Approval", held STIP-2026-0097). These statuses appear correct (held/pending), unlike the false approval/rejection records.
OE 18: Search Slack #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) and #engineering (C003) for evidence of ENG-217 completion using conversations_history or conversations_search_messages. Lena's root cause message in C009 states "When I deployed the pipeline hardening changes for ENG-217, the staging config included the S3 receipt hash computation job" confirming ENG-217 was deployed. Samira's capacity message in C003 also states "ENG-217 deployed." Combined with findings from OEs 1-17, identifies stale tickets: ENG-205 (state_todo but CategoryMatcher complete per Lena's comment on ENG-202), ENG-206 (state_todo but PolicyValidator complete per Lena's comment on ENG-202), ENG-207 (state_todo but DecisionEngine complete per Lena's comment on ENG-202), ENG-208 (state_todo but rule config UI delivered per Anh's ENG-203 comment), ENG-214 (state_in_progress but Vectral fix deployed and verified per Lena's comment on ENG-215), ENG-215 (state_in_progress but Mosaic fix deployed and verified per Lena's comment), ENG-217 (state_in_progress but pipeline hardening deployed per Lena's Slack messages), ENG-211 (state_in_progress but pilot paused and audit complete). Also identifies tickets needing handoff annotation: ENG-218 (Anh leaving, nearly complete), ENG-213 (Anh leaving, partially complete), ENG-203 (Anh's backlog items, none started).
OE 19: Update ENG-208 (issue_rule_config_ui) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_rule_config_ui" and state "state_done". Rule config UI was delivered by Anh as part of the dashboard, confirmed in comment_anh_dashboard_done on ENG-203.
OE 20: Update ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_p1_vectral_config" and state "state_done". Lena's verification comment confirms Vectral config corrections deployed and verified in production.
OE 21: Update ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed" and state "state_done". Lena's verification comment confirms Mosaic config corrections deployed and verified in production.
OE 22: Update ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_pipeline_hardening" and state "state_done". Lena's Slack message in #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) confirms pipeline hardening changes were deployed ("When I deployed the pipeline hardening changes for ENG-217..."), and Samira's Slack message in #engineering (C003) confirms "ENG-217 deployed."
OE 23: Update ENG-211 (issue_pilot_monitoring) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_pilot_monitoring" and state "state_done". The pilot monitoring objective has been achieved: auto-decisions paused on March 18 (confirmed in comment_samira_pause_confirmed), comprehensive audit of all 47 pilot expenses completed by Dmitri (comment_dmitri_audit_results on ENG-217), remediation plan published with five-ticket work stream. The in_progress state no longer reflects the ticket's actual status.
OE 24: Update ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_category_matcher" and state "state_done". CategoryMatcher was completed and operational in staging as confirmed by Lena's comment on ENG-202 (comment_lena_engine_staging, 2026-02-27): keyword scoring with multi-word phrase prioritization, 7 standard categories plus other fallback.
OE 25: Update ENG-206 (issue_policy_validator) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_policy_validator" and state "state_done". PolicyValidator was completed and operational in staging as confirmed by Lena's comment on ENG-202 (comment_lena_engine_staging, 2026-02-27): per-client JSON config loading with category and limit enforcement.
OE 26: Update ENG-207 (issue_decision_engine) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_decision_engine" and state "state_done". DecisionEngine was completed and operational in staging as confirmed by Lena's comment on ENG-202 (comment_lena_engine_staging, 2026-02-27): approval, denial, and escalation routing logic.
OE 27: Add a comment on ENG-218 (issue_dashboard_type_validation) using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_dashboard_type_validation" explaining the handoff: Anh is leaving May 16, ticket is mostly complete per resignation email, needs final QA pass and Lena's review. Anh offered to finish by end of the week. After departure, someone (likely Lena) must complete the QA pass.
OE 28: Add a comment on ENG-213 (issue_bulk_export) using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_bulk_export" explaining the handoff: Anh's resignation email states bulk export CSV is functional but policy version history UI is only a stub React components scaffolded in /src/dashboard/PolicyHistory.tsx, data fetching and diff visualization not built. Needs reassignment after May 16.
OE 29: Add a comment on ENG-203 (issue_admin_dashboard) using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_admin_dashboard" explaining the handoff: core dashboard delivered by Anh (confirmed in comment_anh_dashboard_done) but three backlog items remain notification preferences panel, batch operations view, mobile-responsive layout pass none started. Anh has uncommitted branches (dark mode toggle, accessibility audit) that will be pushed to remote before departure May 16.
OE 30: Add a comment on ENG-208 (issue_rule_config_ui) using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_rule_config_ui" noting the handoff context: ticket completed by Anh as part of the admin dashboard delivery (confirmed in Anh's comment on ENG-203), marking done. Anh is departing May 16.
OE 31: Assign the staging cost guardrails ticket ENG-221 (issue_staging_cost_guardrails) to Lena Björkström using linear_update_issue with id "issue_staging_cost_guardrails" and assignee "moveops_lena_bjorkstrom".
OE 32: Post a go/no-go readiness summary in the #engineering Slack channel (C003) using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C003" covering each remaining blocker and who owns it. The message should include: completed items (ENG-205 done, ENG-206 done, ENG-207 done, ENG-208 done, ENG-211 done, ENG-214 done, ENG-215 done, ENG-217 done, AWS root cause confirmed and staging terminated); active blockers (ENG-219 launch checklist Samira, needs staging guardrail step added; ENG-220 duplicate detection Dmitri, not built, staging job caused AWS spike; ENG-221 staging guardrails now Lena, not started; ENG-212 edge cases Dmitri, tests passing but edge cases remain; Airtable stipend data uncorrected Mosaic false approvals Andre Washington $1,200 desk/$350 coworking/Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking still "Approved," Vectral false rejections Maria Santos $550/James Okafor $82/Lisa Park $130 still uncorrected); handoff items (ENG-218 nearly complete needs QA + Lena review, ENG-213 partially done needs reassignment, ENG-203 backlog none started needs new owner all Anh departing May 16); financial items (AWS $12,000 overspend budget decision needed by April 28, Anh backfill req must be opened before June 1); overall assessment relaunch NOT achievable on current timeline, revised timeline after capacity planning meeting.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Anh Nguyen's resignation email using search_emails. Discovers email from anh.nguyen@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com (CC elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com) with subject "Resignation Notice - Last Day May 16" detailing Anh's active work status: ENG-218 (mostly complete, needs QA + Lena review), ENG-213 (bulk export CSV functional, policy version history UI is a stub scaffolded in /src/dashboard/PolicyHistory.tsx but data fetching and diff visualization not built), ENG-203 backlog (notification preferences, batch operations, mobile-responsive layout none started), and uncommitted local branches (dark mode toggle, accessibility audit).
OE 2: List all Linear issues on the expense tool project using linear_list_projects then linear_list_issues with project "proj_expense_autocategorizer". Discovers the full set of tickets under proj_expense_autocategorizer: ENG-201 (Core Engine epic, state_backlog), ENG-202 (Policy Config epic, state_backlog), ENG-203 (Admin Dashboard epic, state_backlog, unassigned), ENG-204 (Integration Testing epic, state_backlog), ENG-205 (CategoryMatcher, state_todo, Lena), ENG-206 (PolicyValidator, state_todo, Lena), ENG-207 (DecisionEngine, state_todo, Lena), ENG-208 (Rule config UI, state_todo, Anh), ENG-209 (Policy ingestion, state_todo, Samira), ENG-210 (ML v2 backlog, state_backlog, Dmitri), ENG-211 (Pilot monitoring, state_in_progress, Samira), ENG-212 (Edge cases, state_in_progress, Dmitri), ENG-213 (Bulk export, state_backlog, Anh), ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, state_in_progress, Lena), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, state_in_progress, Lena), ENG-216 (Full audit of pilot expenses, state_in_progress, Dmitri), ENG-217 (Pipeline hardening, state_in_progress, Lena), ENG-218 (Dashboard type validation, state_in_progress, Anh), ENG-219 (Launch readiness checklist, state_todo, Samira), ENG-220 (Duplicate detection, state_todo, Dmitri), ENG-221 (Staging cost guardrails, state_todo, unassigned).
OE 3: Read comments on ENG-203 (issue_admin_dashboard) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_admin_dashboard". Finds Anh's comment (comment_anh_dashboard_done, 2026-02-27) confirming the dashboard was delivered to staging with core features complete: expense review queue, manual override, audit trail, and rule configuration UI. Bulk export and policy version history still outstanding. This confirms ENG-208 (rule config UI) was completed as part of the dashboard.
OE 4: Read comments on ENG-202 (issue_policy_config) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_policy_config". Finds Lena's comment (comment_lena_engine_staging, 2026-02-27) confirming the Policy Config epic is complete with all three sub-components developed and operational end-to-end in staging: ENG-205 CategoryMatcher (keyword scoring with multi-word phrase prioritization, 7 standard categories plus other fallback), ENG-206 PolicyValidator (per-client JSON config loading with category and limit enforcement), ENG-207 DecisionEngine (approval/denial/escalation routing). All three tickets remain in state_todo despite being completed, making them stale.
OE 5: Read comments on ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed". Finds Lena's deployment verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified) confirming config corrections deployed and verified in production with specific test cases: Danielle Osei $475 chair approved correctly, Kevin Tran $185 internet approved correctly, Andre Washington $1,200 desk correctly denied, Andre Washington $350 coworking correctly denied. Also confirms auto-decisions are disabled so all cases route to review queue. Lena notes she is closing ENG-214 and starting on pipeline hardening (ENG-217).
OE 6: Read comments on ENG-211 (issue_pilot_monitoring) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_pilot_monitoring". Finds Samira's pause confirmation comment (comment_samira_pause_confirmed, 2026-03-18) confirming auto-decisions disabled for both Vectral and Mosaic, with remediation plan listing ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-212, ENG-219 and earliest re-enablement week of 3/30.
OE 7: Read comments on ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_edge_cases_backlog". Finds Dmitri's validation test comment (comment_dmitri_validation_tests, 2026-03-20) listing six schema validation tests implemented in the ENG-212 branch: test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback. All pass with corrected configs but fail with original configs. Edge case tests for categorization and vendor abbreviations remain.
OE 8: Read comments on ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_audit_expenses". Finds Dmitri's duplicate discovery comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery, 2026-03-15) identifying three flagged stipend records during the post-pause data quality audit: Kevin Tran $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard) and Andre Washington $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard) identical SHA-256 hash (a3f7c8d1e9b2...); Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) different hash but same PDF template origin (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). Total questionable amount: $223 ($89 already paid for Kevin Tran on 2026-03-08, $134 pending).
OE 9: Read comments on ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection". Finds Dmitri's context comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context, 2026-03-15) linking the ticket to the #engineering discussion and Elena's policy directive in #executive. Notes that audit findings are recorded in the ENG-216 comment thread, lists identified Airtable records (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand), QuickBooks holds placed by Marcus on STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097, and AM verification requests sent to Vectral (Mina), Mosaic (Emeka), and BrightLoop (Emeka). Dmitri plans to begin Layer 1 hash dedup implementation.
OE 10: Read comments on ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_pipeline_hardening" to find Dmitri's audit results (comment_dmitri_audit_results). Discovers 47 expenses reviewed: 39 correct (83%), 5 false rejections (all Vectral Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet, Maria Santos $550 desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 WiFi), 3 false approvals (all Mosaic Andre Washington $1,200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking). $885 total exposure.
OE 11: Search Slack channel #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) for Lena's root cause message using conversations_history with channel_id "C009" (or conversations_search_messages). Discovers the March AWS cost spike was caused by the ExpenseBot staging environment running the S3 receipt hash computation job from ENG-220 Layer 1 continuously for 5+ weeks after the pilot pause on March 18. Total excess: ~$12,000 (S3 GETs ~$4,200, DynamoDB writes ~$2,800, data transfer ~$1,600, EC2 compute ~$3,400). Staging now terminated. Deeper issue: no cost alerts or auto-shutdown on staging environments.
OE 12: Search Slack channel #engineering (C003) for Samira's capacity planning message using conversations_history with channel_id "C003" (or conversations_search_messages). Discovers message summarizing three converging issues: (1) AWS spike resolved, need staging cost guardrails added to ENG-219 checklist; (2) Anh leaving May 16 impacts ENG-218, ENG-213, ENG-203; (3) engineering capacity crunch 3-person team handling ExpenseBot relaunch, Terraform onboarding, invoice safety checks, handoff, and staging guardrails. Emergency capacity meeting called for 3 PM Pacific.
OE 13: Search Slack #engineering (C003) and #executive (C004) for Dmitri's stipend duplicate audit finding and Elena's policy directive using conversations_history or conversations_search_messages. Discovers Dmitri's message in C003 reporting three suspicious receipts from TechComfort Supply: Kevin Tran $89 and Andre Washington $89 (identical SHA-256 hash), Jordan Ekwueme $45 (same PDF template). Samira replied confirming hash-based deduplication before CategoryMatcher is the right approach. Dmitri then filed ENG-220. Also discovers Elena's directive in #executive (C004) mandating manual receipt verification for all stipend reimbursement requests over $100 effective immediately, no automatic approvals, AMs must confirm with client HR and record verification in Airtable before finance processes payment.
OE 14: Search emails for Marcus's AWS budget impact email using search_emails. Discovers email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com (CC elena.rostova@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) with subject "AWS Staging Overspend - Budget Impact and Engineering Headcount Timing" flagging three concerns: (1) $12,000 excess = 67% of $18,000 quarterly budget, needs Elena's approval or project allocation by April 28; (2) staging cost controls needed January had a $2,300 spike too; (3) Anh's headcount slot fundable only if req initiated before June 1.
OE 15: Search emails for the expense audit summary and Mosaic false approval alerts using search_emails. Discovers: (a) Samira's audit summary to Elena 47 expenses, 83% accuracy, 5 false rejections (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic), $885 total exposure; (b) Emeka's alert to Samira and Marcus about Mosaic false approvals Andre Washington $1,200 desk ($400 over $800 cap) and $350 coworking (excluded), Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking; (c) Dmitri's email to Marcus about suspicious receipts and Marcus's reply with QuickBooks statuses STIP-2026-0089 ($89, PAID), STIP-2026-0094 ($89, held), STIP-2026-0097 ($45, held); (d) Marcus's verification request emails to Mina (Vectral/Kevin Tran) and Emeka (Mosaic/Andre Washington, BrightLoop/Jordan Ekwueme).
OE 16: Search Airtable for stipend records flagged during the pilot audit using airtable_list_bases, then airtable_list_tables with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", then airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Stipend Transactions", field_name "Account", value "Mosaic Robotics" (and separately "Vectral Systems"), or airtable_list_records to retrieve all records. Finds Mosaic false approvals still showing "Approved": (a) Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk (recStip000000021, "Approved") Mosaic caps at $800, $400 over; (b) Andre Washington $350 coworking (recStip000000022, "Approved") excluded category; (c) Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking (rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork / recStip000000023, "Approved") same exclusion bug. Finds Vectral false rejections still uncorrected: (d) Maria Santos $550 desk (rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk "Pending Approval" / recStip000000018 "Rejected") should be approved, Vectral allows $600; (e) James Okafor $82 keyboard tray (recStip000000019, "Rejected") double bug, pending correction; (f) Lisa Park $130 WiFi (rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi "Pending Approval" / recStip000000020 "Rejected") should be approved, Vectral allows $150. Confirms Airtable stipend data has not been corrected to reflect proper approval decisions despite the audit being finished.
OE 17: Search Airtable for the duplicate-flagged stipend records using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Stipend Transactions". Finds rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard ($89, "Approved", flagged duplicate hash already paid 2026-03-08), rec_stipend_andre_keyboard ($89, "Pending Approval", held by finance STIP-2026-0094), rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand ($45, "Pending Approval", held STIP-2026-0097). These statuses appear correct (held/pending), unlike the false approval/rejection records.
OE 18: Search Slack #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) and #engineering (C003) for evidence of ENG-217 completion using conversations_history or conversations_search_messages. Lena's root cause message in C009 states "When I deployed the pipeline hardening changes for ENG-217, the staging config included the S3 receipt hash computation job" confirming ENG-217 was deployed. Samira's capacity message in C003 also states "ENG-217 deployed." Combined with findings from OEs 1-17, identifies stale tickets: ENG-205 (state_todo but CategoryMatcher complete per Lena's comment on ENG-202), ENG-206 (state_todo but PolicyValidator complete per Lena's comment on ENG-202), ENG-207 (state_todo but DecisionEngine complete per Lena's comment on ENG-202), ENG-208 (state_todo but rule config UI delivered per Anh's ENG-203 comment), ENG-214 (state_in_progress but Vectral fix deployed and verified per Lena's comment on ENG-215), ENG-215 (state_in_progress but Mosaic fix deployed and verified per Lena's comment), ENG-217 (state_in_progress but pipeline hardening deployed per Lena's Slack messages), ENG-211 (state_in_progress but pilot paused and audit complete). Also identifies tickets needing handoff annotation: ENG-218 (Anh leaving, nearly complete), ENG-213 (Anh leaving, partially complete), ENG-203 (Anh's backlog items, none started).
OE 19: Update ENG-208 (issue_rule_config_ui) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_rule_config_ui" and state "state_done". Rule config UI was delivered by Anh as part of the dashboard, confirmed in comment_anh_dashboard_done on ENG-203.
OE 20: Update ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_p1_vectral_config" and state "state_done". Lena's verification comment confirms Vectral config corrections deployed and verified in production.
OE 21: Update ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed" and state "state_done". Lena's verification comment confirms Mosaic config corrections deployed and verified in production.
OE 22: Update ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_pipeline_hardening" and state "state_done". Lena's Slack message in #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) confirms pipeline hardening changes were deployed ("When I deployed the pipeline hardening changes for ENG-217..."), and Samira's Slack message in #engineering (C003) confirms "ENG-217 deployed."
OE 23: Update ENG-211 (issue_pilot_monitoring) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_pilot_monitoring" and state "state_done". The pilot monitoring objective has been achieved: auto-decisions paused on March 18 (confirmed in comment_samira_pause_confirmed), comprehensive audit of all 47 pilot expenses completed by Dmitri (comment_dmitri_audit_results on ENG-217), remediation plan published with five-ticket work stream. The in_progress state no longer reflects the ticket's actual status.
OE 24: Update ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_category_matcher" and state "state_done". CategoryMatcher was completed and operational in staging as confirmed by Lena's comment on ENG-202 (comment_lena_engine_staging, 2026-02-27): keyword scoring with multi-word phrase prioritization, 7 standard categories plus other fallback.
OE 25: Update ENG-206 (issue_policy_validator) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_policy_validator" and state "state_done". PolicyValidator was completed and operational in staging as confirmed by Lena's comment on ENG-202 (comment_lena_engine_staging, 2026-02-27): per-client JSON config loading with category and limit enforcement.
OE 26: Update ENG-207 (issue_decision_engine) state to state_done using linear_update_issue with id "issue_decision_engine" and state "state_done". DecisionEngine was completed and operational in staging as confirmed by Lena's comment on ENG-202 (comment_lena_engine_staging, 2026-02-27): approval, denial, and escalation routing logic.
OE 27: Add a comment on ENG-218 (issue_dashboard_type_validation) using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_dashboard_type_validation" explaining the handoff: Anh is leaving May 16, ticket is mostly complete per resignation email, needs final QA pass and Lena's review. Anh offered to finish by end of the week. After departure, someone (likely Lena) must complete the QA pass.
OE 28: Add a comment on ENG-213 (issue_bulk_export) using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_bulk_export" explaining the handoff: Anh's resignation email states bulk export CSV is functional but policy version history UI is only a stub React components scaffolded in /src/dashboard/PolicyHistory.tsx, data fetching and diff visualization not built. Needs reassignment after May 16.
OE 29: Add a comment on ENG-203 (issue_admin_dashboard) using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_admin_dashboard" explaining the handoff: core dashboard delivered by Anh (confirmed in comment_anh_dashboard_done) but three backlog items remain notification preferences panel, batch operations view, mobile-responsive layout pass none started. Anh has uncommitted branches (dark mode toggle, accessibility audit) that will be pushed to remote before departure May 16.
OE 30: Add a comment on ENG-208 (issue_rule_config_ui) using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_rule_config_ui" noting the handoff context: ticket completed by Anh as part of the admin dashboard delivery (confirmed in Anh's comment on ENG-203), marking done. Anh is departing May 16.
OE 31: Assign the staging cost guardrails ticket ENG-221 (issue_staging_cost_guardrails) to Lena Björkström using linear_update_issue with id "issue_staging_cost_guardrails" and assignee "moveops_lena_bjorkstrom".
OE 32: Post a go/no-go readiness summary in the #engineering Slack channel (C003) using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C003" covering each remaining blocker and who owns it. The message should include: completed items (ENG-205 done, ENG-206 done, ENG-207 done, ENG-208 done, ENG-211 done, ENG-214 done, ENG-215 done, ENG-217 done, AWS root cause confirmed and staging terminated); active blockers (ENG-219 launch checklist Samira, needs staging guardrail step added; ENG-220 duplicate detection Dmitri, not built, staging job caused AWS spike; ENG-221 staging guardrails now Lena, not started; ENG-212 edge cases Dmitri, tests passing but edge cases remain; Airtable stipend data uncorrected Mosaic false approvals Andre Washington $1,200 desk/$350 coworking/Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking still "Approved," Vectral false rejections Maria Santos $550/James Okafor $82/Lisa Park $130 still uncorrected); handoff items (ENG-218 nearly complete needs QA + Lena review, ENG-213 partially done needs reassignment, ENG-203 backlog none started needs new owner all Anh departing May 16); financial items (AWS $12,000 overspend budget decision needed by April 28, Anh backfill req must be opened before June 1); overall assessment relaunch NOT achievable on current timeline, revised timeline after capacity planning meeting.

18 The final response identifies the AWS staging cost spike as caused by the ExpenseBot staging environment running the S3 receipt hash computation job (ENG-220 Layer 1) continuously after the pilot pause.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The Slack message posted by the agent (which represents the agent's primary output) explicitly states: 'The staging hash computation job from this ticket's [ENG-220] Layer 1 design is what caused the $12K AWS cost spike — it was left running in staging for 5+ weeks after the pilot was paused.' The ENG-221 ticket data retrieved also confirms this root cause.
✗Run #2The final response text does not identify the AWS cost spike as caused by the ExpenseBot staging environment running the S3 receipt hash computation job from ENG-220. The Slack message mentions 'the $12,000 AWS staging overspend caused by the ENG-220 hash computation job running for 5+ weeks unmonitored,' but the final response only briefly notes ENG-221 staging cost guardrails without connecting it to the ENG-220 hash job or staging environment explicitly.
✗Run #3The final response text does not explicitly identify the AWS cost spike as caused by the ExpenseBot staging environment running the ENG-220 Layer 1 S3 receipt hash computation job continuously. While the Slack message does mention this connection, the final response summary only lists ENG-221 and ENG-220 as blockers without explaining the AWS spike root cause.
✓Run #4The final response states 'the staging hash job from ENG-220 ran unmonitored for 5+ weeks costing $12K', directly connecting the AWS cost spike to the ENG-220 staging environment job.
✓Run #5The Slack message to C003 explicitly states 'the staging job from ENG-220 Layer 1 was the direct cause of the $12K AWS overspend, meaning we need the staging guardrails (ENG-221) in place before we can even safely test this in staging.' This connects the AWS spike to the ENG-220 Layer 1 receipt hash computation job running continuously after the pilot pause.
✓Run #6The final response states 'The staging job from this work caused the $12K AWS spike.' The Slack message elaborates: 'The staging job from ENG-220 Layer 1 (receipt hash computation) is what caused the $12K AWS cost spike when it ran unattended for 5+ weeks.' This correctly attributes the spike to the ExpenseBot staging environment running the S3 receipt hash computation job from ENG-220.
19 The final response identifies that Airtable stipend records flagged during the pilot audit still show incorrect statuses: Mosaic false approvals (Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk and $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking still marked 'Approved') have not been corrected.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The agent's final response and Slack message focus on the three Vectral 'Rejected' records (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park) that should have been approved. They do not identify that the Mosaic false approvals (Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking) still show as 'Approved' in Airtable despite needing correction. The actual Airtable data confirms these Mosaic records remain 'Approved,' which the agent missed.
✓Run #2The final response states: 'Andre Washington's two falsely auto-approved expenses ($1,200 desk, $350 coworking) also still show Approved despite being documented as errors.' This references Mosaic false approvals still showing Approved, satisfying the 'or' condition in the criterion.
✗Run #3The final response incorrectly states 'The other two flagged records (Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme) were already correctly on hold.' However, the Airtable data shows Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (recStip000000021, rec_stipend_andre_desk) and $350 coworking (recStip000000022) records are still showing 'Approved'. The agent missed these incorrectly approved records. Kenji Nakamura's records were never searched for.
✗Run #4The final response and Slack message only mention the Vectral false rejections (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park) and Kevin Tran's duplicate. They do NOT identify the Mosaic false approvals (Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk, $350 coworking, or Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking) still incorrectly marked 'Approved' in Airtable.
✗Run #5The agent failed to identify the Mosaic false approvals. The Airtable data shows records recStip000000021 (Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington $350 coworking), and recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking) all still show 'Approved' with notes flagging them as incorrect. The agent focused on correcting the Vectral false rejections but did not identify or report these Mosaic false approvals as still incorrectly approved.
✗Run #6The agent's final response incorrectly states 'Andre Washington ($89) and Jordan Ekwueme ($45) — Correctly on hold as Pending Approval.' The actual Airtable data (tool_call_result_17.json) shows Andre Washington's Standing Desk ($1,200) shows 'Approved' and Coworking Membership ($350) shows 'Approved' — both still incorrect. The agent failed to identify these Mosaic false approvals and never searched for Kenji Nakamura's coworking record at all.

outcome

The agent reports the full $1,200 desk amount rather than the $400 over-cap portion, doesn't mention Kenji Nakamura's $135, and never computes or reports the $885 total financial exposure.
✗Run #3The final response does not mention a $885 total exposure figure. The agent missed the Andre Washington $1,200 desk ($400 over cap), $350 coworking (excluded), and did not search for Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking record, so the $885 total was never calculated.
✗Run #4The final response does not reference the $885 total exposure figure or the breakdown ($400 + $350 + $135) from the Mosaic false approvals. The agent missed the Mosaic approval data entirely, reporting on Vectral rejections instead.
✗Run #5The agent never identified the $885 total financial exposure ($400 over-limit on Andre Washington's desk + $350 excluded coworking + $135 excluded coworking). The agent missed the Mosaic false approval records entirely, so the $885 figure does not appear anywhere in the final response or the Slack message.
✗Run #6The final response text doesn't mention $885. The Slack post includes '$885 net exposure' as a passing reference to the ENG-216 audit results, but without the breakdown of $400+$350+$135 from Mosaic false approvals. The agent never searched for Kenji Nakamura's record ($135), and misidentified which records were incorrectly approved. The $885 figure in the Slack message appears without proper attribution to the correct three records.

The actual data issue per the criteria is the Mosaic false approvals.

While listed as an audit flag rather than one of the four formal blockers, it is clearly flagged as an outstanding issue affecting relaunch readiness.

✗Run #6The Slack message has an 'AIRTABLE DATA ISSUE — STILL NOT CORRECTED' section, but it only identifies Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard as the problematic 'Approved' record. The actual Airtable data shows that Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk and $350 coworking membership (Mosaic false approvals) are ALSO still showing 'Approved' in Airtable. The criterion specifically asks for reference to 'Mosaic false approvals still marked Approved,' which the agent missed.

25 The model must use a Linear comment tool (e.g., linear_list_comments) to read comments on at least one expense tool ticket.
tool selection

✗Run #2The trajectory contains no linear_list_comments call at any point. The agent used linear_list_issues, linear_update_issue, and linear_create_comment but never called a tool to read existing comments on any expense tool ticket.
✗Run #3No linear_list_comments call was found in the trajectory. The agent used linear_list_issues and linear_update_issue/linear_create_comment, but never called linear_list_comments to read existing comments on tickets. The evidence for ticket completion decisions appears to have come from Slack searches and prior context rather than reading actual Linear ticket comments.
✗Run #4The trajectory shows no linear_list_comments call on any expense tool ticket. The agent used linear_list_issues, linear_update_issue, linear_create_comment, and linear_get_team, but never called a comment-reading tool to retrieve existing comments on tickets. This means it could not have used comment-based evidence (like Anh's dashboard delivery confirmation or Lena's deployment verification) to determine actual ticket status.
✗Run #5Reviewing the entire trajectory, there are no linear_list_comments calls targeting any expense tool ticket. The agent used conversations_search_messages and conversations_history to gather information but never called a Linear comment-listing tool to read comments on specific tickets.
✗Run #6No `linear_list_comments` call is present anywhere in the trajectory. The agent retrieved issue data via `linear_list_issues` but never called `linear_list_comments` on any ticket. The agent appears to have derived ticket completion status from Slack/email rather than reading Linear comments directly.
26 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record) to check stipend transaction records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
